headline: French PM resolves to step up economic aid as Middle East conflict drags on
description: Sébastien Lecornu plans to unveil new measures to support economic activity. He said the sustained rise in oil prices means France must 'change scale,' even if it makes the country's already tough budget situation more complicated.

headline: Plaintiffs' lawyers denounce 'inversion of victimhood' at Sarkozy's appeal trial
French shipping firm says vessel targeted in Strait of Hormuz
On Algeria, Macron has a goal for his last year in office: Reconciliation
description: The French president, who began his first term aiming to ease historical tensions, now faces the most severe crisis in bilateral relations since Algeria's independence in 1962.

headline: Sweden's conservatives are now normalizing the far-right
description: With legislative elections scheduled for September 13 approaching, the Conservatives, Liberals and Christian Democrats have signaled their willingness to govern with the Sweden Democrats, the far-right party that enabled them to return to power in 2022.

headline: German Chancellor Merz hits a new low one year after taking office
description: At the helm of a coalition plagued by disagreements and criticized for his verbal gaffes, the German head of government is the most unpopular chancellor since the founding of the Federal Republic.

headline: Scottish elections: Reform UK capitalizes on anti-migrant protests in Falkirk
description: As regional elections take place on Thursday, May 7, in Scotland and Wales, far-right activists have managed to rally broad support around slogans hostile to asylum seekers in a region previously known for being welcoming.

Louvre President Christophe Leribault: 'I am not here to undo the work of my predecessors'
description: In an interview, the successor to Laurence des Cars as head of the museum explains how he intends to make his mark with humility, courage and ambition.

headline: The 'alpha male' holds little appeal for women (and comes at a high cost)
description: While many young men are receptive to the hyper-masculine rhetoric that is spread across social media, the stereotypes touted as ways to attract women rarely succeed, writes columnist Maïa Mazaurette. Instead, she encourages men to take a genuine interest in women.

headline: Hundreds of flight cancellations leave passengers frustrated and stranded
description: The war in the Middle East has caused the price of jet fuel to double in just a few weeks. Many airlines, especially low-cost carriers, have canceled flights with low occupancy or low profitability. Passenger rights are protected, but within certain limits.

headline: 'OPEC, founded on a desire for sovereignty, is now fracturing in the name of that same sovereignty'
